name: University of Bamenda
about: The University of Bamenda (UBa) was created by Presidential Decree N°2010/372 of 14 December 2010. Its vision is to be a leading institution of higher learning addressing present and future development challenges through efficient teaching, research, and innovation.
missions: Provide quality needs-based teaching and research
Instill lifelong integrity and practical intellectual confidence
Respond to national and international development challenges
Cultivate Anglo-Saxon values without sacrificing cultural diversity

name: University of Buea
about: Admission conditions are generally the same across the University of Buea except for certain faculties and professional schools that require competitive entrance examinations.
missions: Provide world-class Anglophone higher education
Develop research in science, medicine and technology
Foster community engagement and sustainable development
Build bridges between Anglophone and Francophone academic traditions

name: University of Dschang
about: The University of Dschang was created by Decree N°93/026 of January 19, 1993 following the university reform in Cameroon. It evolved from the former University Center of Dschang.
missions: Lead agricultural and environmental research in Central Africa
Train professionals in agronomy, forestry and natural resources
Develop legal, economic and humanities education
Promote decentralization through satellite campuses

name: University of Douala
about: The University of Douala was created on April 13, 1992 after the transformation of the University Center of Douala into a university. It operates across several campuses in Douala.
missions: Train high-level professionals adapted to the economic environment
Develop research in partnership with industry and civil society
Promote entrepreneurship and business innovation
Foster bilingual education in Cameroon's economic hub

name: University of Ngaoundéré
about: The University of Ngaoundéré was created by Presidential Decree N°93/028 of January 19, 1993 following the transformation of the University Center of Ngaoundéré into a university.
missions: Develop agro-industrial and food sciences research
Train engineers for northern Cameroon's development
Foster veterinary medicine and animal health research
Bridge academic knowledge with the needs of the Adamawa region

name: University of Maroua
about: The University of Maroua was created by Decree N°2008/280 of August 9, 2008. The institution comprises five faculties and two major schools.
missions: Expand higher education access to the Far North Region
Train specialists in mines, petroleum and natural resources
Develop sciences and humanities for sustainable development
Promote teacher training and vocational education

name: University of Garoua
about: The University of Garoua was created by Decree N°2022/003 of January 5, 2022 and includes several faculties and higher schools.
missions: Extend higher education to the North Region
Develop medical and biomedical sciences education
Train professionals in economics, law and fine arts
Promote educational sciences and teacher training

name: University of Yaoundé I
about: The University of Yaoundé I is located at Ngoa Ekelle in Yaoundé, the political capital of Cameroon, and hosts several faculties and major schools.
missions: Provide quality teaching and research across all domains
Foster scientific innovation and technological development
Train the next generation of skilled professionals and researchers
Maintain strong international academic partnerships

name: University of Yaoundé II
about: The University of Yaoundé II was created on January 19, 1993 and operates across several campuses including Soa, Ngoa Ekelle, and Obili.
missions: Train legal, economic and political science professionals
Develop expertise in international relations and diplomacy
Foster demographic research and public policy
Promote communication and information sciences

name: University of Bertoua
about: The University of Bertoua was created by Decree N°2022/003 of January 5, 2022 and includes four faculties and four major schools.
missions: Provide higher education access to the East Region
Develop expertise in agriculture, wood and environmental sciences
Train professionals in mining, energy and urban planning
Promote teacher education and vocational training

name: University of Ebolowa
about: The University of Ebolowa was created by Decree N°2022/003 of January 5, 2022 and includes several faculties and higher schools.
missions: Develop medical and pharmaceutical education in southern Cameroon
Train professionals in agriculture and environmental management
Provide legal, economic and arts education for the South Region
Foster technical and transport education

name: Ebenezer Higher Institute of Science and Technology
about: Ebenezer Higher Institute of Science and Technology (EHIST) is a private institution located on Sonac Street, Bamenda. It offers programmes in science and technology disciplines to equip students with practical and theoretical skills for the modern workforce.
missions: Provide quality science and technology education
Develop practical skills for industry and research
Foster innovation and entrepreneurship among students

name: Abege University Institute
about: Abege University Institute is a private higher education institution located at New Road Mile 2, Bamenda. The institute provides accessible quality education to students in the North West Region and beyond.
missions: Offer affordable quality higher education
Provide industry-relevant programmes
Support student professional development

name: Higher Institute of Professional and Management Studies
img: /images/private-university-images/HIGHER INSTITUTE OF PROFESSIONAL AND MANAGEMENt cover image.jpg
logo: /images/private-university-images/HIGHER INSTITUTE OF PROFESSIONAL AND MANAGEMENT logo.jpg
about: The Higher Institute of Professional and Management Studies (HIPMS) is situated on Commercial Avenue, Bamenda. It specialises in professional and management education, preparing graduates for leadership roles in business and public service.
missions: Train competent managers and business leaders
Provide professional and vocational education
Bridge the gap between academic learning and workplace demands

name: Prestige Higher Institute of Professional Training
img: /images/private-university-images/PRESTIGE HIGHER INSTITUTE OF PROFESSIONAL TRAINING cover image.jpg
logo: /images/private-university-images/PRESTIGE HIGHER INSTITUTE OF PROFESSIONAL TRAINING logo.jpg
about: Prestige Higher Institute of Professional Training (PHIPT) is located at Mile 3, Bamenda. It focuses on delivering high-quality professional training programmes that meet the demands of the local and national job market.
missions: Deliver targeted professional training
Partner with industry for relevant curricula
Empower graduates for immediate workforce entry

name: Yaoundé International Business School
img: /images/private-university-images/YAOUNDÉ INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS SCHOOL, YAOUNDÉ cover image.jpg
about: Yaoundé International Business School (YIBS) is a leading private business institution in Yaoundé offering internationally recognised business and management programmes. It aims to produce world-class business professionals from Cameroon.
missions: Provide internationally-benchmarked business education
Foster global business leadership from Africa
Connect students with international markets and networks

name: St Louis University Institute
img: /images/private-university-images/ST LOUIS UNIVERSITY INSTITUTE. BAMENDA, DOUALA, YAOUNDÉ cover image.jpg
logo: /images/private-university-images/ST LOUIS UNIVERSITY INSTITUTE. BAMENDA, DOUALA, YAOUNDÉ logo.jpg
about: St Louis University Institute is a multi-campus private university with locations in Bamenda, Douala, and Yaoundé. It offers a wide range of programmes across disciplines, serving students across Cameroon's major cities.
missions: Provide diverse higher education across multiple campuses
Serve students in Anglophone and Francophone regions
Deliver faith-based values-driven education

name: Catholic University of Cameroon
about: The Catholic University of Cameroon (CATUC), Bamenda, is a private Catholic university established in 2010 and accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education (MINESUP). Located in Mankon, Bamenda, CATUC is guided by the motto 'Fides et Ratio' (Faith and Reason) — embodying its commitment to integrating academic excellence with Catholic values. CATUC offers undergraduate, postgraduate, and professional programmes across six faculties and schools, serving students from across Cameroon and beyond.
missions: Provide quality higher education grounded in Catholic values and academic excellence
Train graduates who are competent, ethical, and socially responsible
Foster research and innovation relevant to African development needs
Promote integral human formation through faith, reason, and service

name: University of Ilorin
img: /images/schools-abroad/University of ilorin , kwara state cover image.jpg
about: The University of Ilorin (UNILORIN) is a federal university located in Ilorin, Kwara State, Nigeria. It is one of Nigeria's premier universities, known for its strong programmes in medicine, engineering, law, and arts.
missions: Advance learning through teaching and research
Foster intellectual and personal development
Contribute to national and regional development

name: Veritas University
about: Veritas University, Abuja is a Catholic University established by the Catholic Bishops' Conference of Nigeria. It offers rigorous academic programmes alongside a strong values and ethics framework.
missions: Provide faith-based quality higher education
Promote integrity, excellence, and service
Contribute to the development of Nigeria and Africa

name: Kampala University
img: /images/schools-abroad/Kampala University Uganda, Ggaba state cover image.jpg
about: Kampala University (KU) is a private university located in Ggaba, Kampala, Uganda. It offers undergraduate and postgraduate programmes across various disciplines and welcomes international students from across Africa.
missions: Provide accessible and quality higher education in East Africa
Promote regional integration through academic exchange
Develop research relevant to African development challenges
